popularStory.description:
Birth: Unknown say 1528 in probably Sidbury, Devon, England.

Death: After 1589 when Richard was last listed as the sole Sidbury Clapp in the subsidy rolls and before 25 JUL 1609 when his widow wrote …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Clapp於1528年出生在英國英格蘭戴文希德波利時，他的父親William Clapp28歲，母親Alice26歲。他於1547年5月8日與Christian Uln在英國戴文布藍斯可姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1609年7月25日之前在英國戴文希德波利，埋葬在英國戴文希德波利。

nameMeanings.name: Clapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): either from Middle English clop(pe), clap(pe) ‘lump’, perhaps denoting a stocky person, or Middle English clap(pe) ‘loud noise; noisy, idle chatter’.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Slovenian Klep: nickname from klep ‘sharpness of scythe or sickle’, also ‘sharpening with a hammer’.

variants.name: Klapp
meanings: German: nickname for a gossip or slanderer, from Middle High German klapf, klaff ‘prattle, malicious gossip’. Compare Clapp .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Clapp was born in 1528, in Sidbury, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Clapp, was 28 and his mother, Alice, was 26. He married Christian Uln on 8 May 1547, in Branscombe, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died before 25 July 1609, in Sidbury, Devon, England, and was buried in Sidbury, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Clapp est né en 1528, à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Clapp, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Alice, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Christian Uln le 8 mai 1547, à Branscombe, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé avant 25 juillet 1609, à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Sidbury, Devon, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Clapp(이)가 1528년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 시드버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Clapp(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1547년 5월 8일 영국 데폰 브랜스코브에서 Christian Uln(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1609년 7월 25일 이전 영국 데폰 시드버이에서 사망했으며 영국 데폰 시드버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Clapp родился в 1528 г. в местности сидбури, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Clapp было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Christian Uln 8 мая 1547 г. в местности бранскомб, девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер перед 25 июля 1609 г. в местности сидбури, девон, Англия и был похоронен в местности сидбури, девон, Англия.
